text: This Engadget article reports that the US, UK and EU formally blamed Russia for a large-scale cyberattack against Viasat’s satellite internet service shortly before the invasion of Ukraine. It states that Viasat said the hacking efforts destroyed tens of thousands of satellite terminals and that officials highlighted impacts on central European users, wind farms and Ukrainian military and civilian customers. The piece recounts comments by Ukrainian cybersecurity authorities on the resulting loss of communications and notes the broader pattern of alleged Russian cyber activities linked to the conflict.
attribution_coding_note: Reports the 'US, UK and EU formally blamed Russia for a large-scale cyberattack' against Viasat.
